title: elymus striatus var. arkansanus
intro: Elymus striatus var. arkansanus, also known as Arkansas wildrye or Arkansas wheatgrass, is a type of grass native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe wounds, reduce pain and inflammation, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Elymus striatus var. arkansanus include its potential to reduce stress, improve digestion, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a tea or tincture, with some herbalists also recommending its use in salves and poultices to aid in wound healing.
